code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "has a height of",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is located in the",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Helper to clean quoted objects and remove extraneous parentheses/units
    import re
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip('"').strip("'")
        # Remove surrounding parentheses but keep inner content
        o = re.sub(r'^\((.*)\)$', r'\1', o)
        # Remove any parentheses within the string, keeping the inner text
        o = re.sub(r'\(([^)]+)\)', r'\1', o)
        # Collapse multiple spaces
        o = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', o).strip()
        return o

    # Build a flat list of clauses, avoiding repeated subjects where possible
    seen_subjects = set()
    clauses = []

    for t in triples:
        subj = clean_obj(t.subject)
        obj = clean_obj(t.object)
        pred = t.predicate

        # Determine the verb phrase
        verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
        # Special phrasing for some predicates
        if pred == "architecturalStyle":
            verb = f"has the {obj} style of architecture"
            clause = f"{subj} {verb}" if subj not in seen_subjects else verb
        elif pred == "buildingStartDate":
            clause = f"Construction of {subj} began in {obj}"
        elif pred == "inaugurationDate":
            clause = f"The inauguration of {subj} was on {obj}"
        elif pred == "leader":
            clause = f"The leader of {subj} is {obj}"
        elif pred == "leaderTitle":
            clause = f"The leader title of {subj} is {obj}"
        elif pred == "capital":
            clause = f"The capital of {subj} is {obj}"
        elif pred == "currency":
            clause = f"The currency of {subj} is {obj}"
        elif pred == "language":
            clause = f"{obj} is spoken in {subj}"
        elif pred == "ethnicGroup":
            clause = f"One ethnic group of {subj} is {obj}"
        elif pred == "origin":
            clause = f"{obj} is the origin of {subj}"
        elif pred == "demonym":
            clause = f"People from {subj} are called {obj}"
        elif pred == "buildingType":
            clause = f"The {subj} is a {obj} type building"
        else:
            # Generic clause
            if subj not in seen_subjects:
                clause = f"{subj} {verb} {obj}"
            else:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj}"

        clauses.append(clause)
        seen_subjects.add(subj)

    # Join all clauses into a single fluent sentence
    final_text = ", ".join(clauses) + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
